title: 9.-Waiver of Sanctuary City Contracting Ordinance to Contract with Superion LLC for FUND$ Software Maintenance and Support

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution waiving the contract prohibition of Chapter 13.105, Sanctuary City
Contracting, in order to continue contracting with Superion LLC (a CentralSquare
company, aka “Superion”) for software maintenance and support of the FUND$ system,
which is critical to the City’s operations.
sections.current_situation:
The City seeks a Council waiver for the Sanctuary City Contracting Ordinance to finalize
a contract with Superion, the sole vendor for the proprietary, mission-critical FUND$
system (30 years of use managing revenue, taxation, and land management).
While Council approved the contract in December 2023 (Res. 71,133-N.S.) and later an
amendment for $592,500 total spending in June 2025 (Res. 71,795-N.S.), the
administrative contract process was never completed. A new contract is required where
one did not previously exist. As part of the contract process, when asked to accept the
City’s updated contract boilerplate, Superion refused to sign the Sanctuary City form.
Superion falls under the scope of the Sanctuary City Contracting Ordinance (BMC
13.105) because it serves Federal agencies, however, when asked if they conduct data
brokering and extreme data vetting to agencies such as ICE, they responded:
"CentralSquare does not currently provide the covered services to ICE. As a public
safety vendor, we cannot agree to preclude ourselves from potentially providing these
services in the future."
Given the lack of any reasonable alternative vendor to maintain this essential platform,
Council approval of a waiver is required under Berkeley Municipal Code Chapter 13.105
to prevent catastrophic disruption to City operations.

CONSENT CALENDAR
October 28, 2025
sections.background:
The City implemented the Superion system in the 1990’s and has since built extensive
processes, data management, and financial workflows around it. The system integrates
deeply into City operations, and replacement requires a multi-year procurement and
implementation effort which is currently underway.
Superion is widely used across state and local governments, as well as by some
Federal agencies. As the proprietary vendor, it retains exclusive rights and ability to
maintain, patch, and support the City’s installation. Because no third party can provide
equivalent services, the City has no alternative but to continue this contract.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
A contract with Superion involves only the provision of software maintenance and
support services. There are no identifiable environmental impacts.
sections.rationale:
The City depends on the FUND$ system to carry out essential financial and land
management operations. No other vendor can provide maintenance and support, and
interruption of services would significantly impair the City’s ability to collect revenue,
manage land records, and administer taxes.
sections.alternatives:
None. No alternative vendors are available, and replacement of the system is not
feasible in the short term. Staff do not recommend continuing to use this critical
software without software maintenance and support.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, (510) 981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ADOPT A RESOLUTION WAIVING THE CONTRACT REQUIREMENTS OF THE
SANCTUARY CITY CONTRACTING ORDINANCE PURSUANT TO CHAPTER 13.105
OF THE BERKELEY MUNICIPAL CODE, IN ORDER TO CONTRACT WITH
SUPERION LLC FOR MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT OF THE CITY’S LEGACY
ENTERPRISE SYSTEM
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley has used the Superion LLC (“Superion”) enterprise
system for more than 30 years to support Accounts Receivable/General Billing, Tax
System, and Land Management operations; and
WHEREAS, Superion is the sole provider of licensing, maintenance, and support for this
proprietary system; and
WHEREAS, no alternative vendor exists that can maintain and support the City’s
existing system, and replacement is not feasible in the short term; and
WHEREAS, pursuant to Ordinance No. 7,650-N.S. and Chapter 13.105, the Sanctuary
City Contracting Ordinance, Council may grant a waiver upon determining that no
reasonable alternative exists; and
WHEREAS, continuation of this contract is essential to maintaining financial stability, tax
collection, and land management functions for the City.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that a
waiver of the “no-contract” provision of BMC Section 13.105 is approved in order for the
City to continue its contract with Superion LLC for software maintenance and support
services.
